MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-16-2003 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
David S. Secrest, Esq., 650/726-7461, for plaintiff Ann Marie Rothman moving for summary judgment
Kristin E. Hutchins, Esq. and Richard N. Hill, Esq. of LITTLER MENDELSON 433-1940, for defendants and opposing party Astra U.S.A., Astra Zeneca LP and Astra Pharmeceuticals, LP
* Plaintiff did not oppose to the Court's tentative ruling. The Court adopted its tentative ruling -- PLAINTIFF ANN MARIE ROTHMAN's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT -- denied. (DAG/301/CL) Reporter: Joe Vickstein, CSR 4780

MOTION Renotice Of Motion For Summary Adjudication Of Issues
Off Calendar Jan-16-2003 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kristin E. Hutchins, Esq. and Richard N. Hill, Esq. of LITTLER MENDELSON 433-1940, for defendants Astra U.S.A., Astra Zeneca LP and Astra Pharmeceuticals, LP, moving for summary adjudication
David S. Secrest, Esq., 650/726-7461, for plaintiff and opposing party Ann Marie Rothman
* A R G U E D and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as modified:
Tentative Ruling:
DEFENDANTs Astra U.S.A., Astra Zeneca LP and Astra Pharmeceuticals, LP's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION --
Re: c/a 1-3: Court withdrew its tentative ruling on the ground that prior dismissal entered.
Re: c/a 4: Granted. No triable issue of fact re: timely tender of wages or improper
calculation re: failure to provide access to personnel files; treated as a JOP and
granted.
Re: c/a 5, 6, 9: Granted. No triable issue of fact re: time Bar. Richards v.
CH2M Hill.
Re: c/a 7: Court withdrew its tentative ruling on the ground that prior dismissal entered.
Re: c/a 10: Denied. Triable issue of fact re: constructive termination, whether Placement
on PIP under circumstances constituted conduct to justify a reasonable person to resign.
Re: c/a 11: Defamation - granted. No triable issue of fact re: privilege or self
publication.
Re: c/a 12: Denied. Triable issue of fact exists re: constructive term and IIED is
appropriately an element of damages, recoverable per c/a 8.
Re: c/a 13 (NIED): Court withdrew its tentative ruling on the ground that prior dismissal entered. Moving party is to prepare an order complying with CCP 437 c(g). (DAG/301/CL) Reporter: Joe Vickstein, CSR 4780
